task: Find the shipping cost of a Common medium-sized box in flat-rate shipping and compare it with other parcel services.
final_result_response:
Based on my research on the UPS website, here are the complete findings:

**UPS Simple Rate - Medium Box (Flat Rate Shipping):**
- **Price: Starting at $16.90**
- **Dimensions:** 251–650 cubic inches
- **Common Box Dimensions:** 8 in × 8 in × 8 in, 12 in × 9 in × 6 in, 13 in × 11 in × 2 in
- **Size Samples:** Shoebox or three-inch, three-ring binder
- **Delivery Speeds Available:** Next-Day, Second Day, Within Three Business Days, Within Five Days

**Comparison with Other Parcel Services (FedEx & USPS):**

| Feature | UPS | FedEx | USPS |
|---|---|---|---|
| Customer Packaging | ✅ Yes | No | No |
| Maximum Volume | Up to 1,728 cubic inches | Up to 1,452 cubic inches | Up to 1,200 cubic inches |
| Shipping Speeds | Five Days, Three Business Days, Second Day, Next-Day | Express Services only (no ground) | Priority Mail 1–3 business days |
| Declared Value | $100 | $100 | $50 |
| Guaranteed | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | No |
| Shipping Rates | National Flat Rate | 3 Zones | National Flat Rate |

**Key UPS Advantages:**
- UPS allows customer packaging (use your own box), while FedEx and USPS require their branded packaging
- UPS offers the highest maximum volume (1,728 cubic inches)
- UPS offers the most delivery speed options (4 speeds)
- UPS uses a single national flat rate (no zone-based pricing like FedEx)
- UPS shipments are guaranteed with $100 declared value
